prevalence.trend: stable to increasing with repetitive work and diabetes prevalence

epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: Clinical symptoms (median nerve distribution) with provocative tests; confirmation by nerve conduction studies when needed; rule out cervical radiculopathy

publicHealthSystem.controlRate: Moderate with splinting and timely decompression when indicated

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: Electrodiagnostic testing access varies; many cases diagnosed clinically in APS/orthopedics
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Activity modification, night splinting, NSAIDs; corticosteroid injection in selected cases; surgical decompression for persistent/severe cases
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Long wait times for nerve conduction studies and surgery in SUS in some regions
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Effective low-cost conservative options and established surgical treatment in referral centers
